put.description: Use when updating customer details (e.g. name, email, or metadata). Identify by id or external_customer_id.

post.description: Use when onboarding a new billing customer (e.g. sign-up or CRM sync). Ideal for linking via external_customer_id to your app's user id.

get.description: Use when resolving a customer by your app's id (e.g. from your user table). Ideal for integrations that key by external id.

post.description: Use when listing or searching customers (e.g. admin CRM or reporting). Returns a paginated list; supports filtering and sorting.

get.description: Use when showing a customer's usage (e.g. portal or overage alerts). Identify by customer_id or customer_lookup_key; supports filters.
Use when you need to load a single customer (e.g. for a billing portal or to attach a subscription).

delete.description: Use when removing a customer (e.g. GDPR or churn). Returns 204 No Content on success.

get.description: Use when checking what a customer can access (e.g. feature gating or usage limits). Supports optional filters (feature_ids, subscription_ids).
Use when showing upcoming or pending credits for a customer (e.g. in a portal or for forecasting).
Use when showing a customer's invoice overview (e.g. billing portal or balance summary). Includes totals and multi-currency breakdown.

post.description: Use when sending a single usage event from your app (e.g. one API call or one GB stored). Events are processed asynchronously; returns 202 with event_id.
Use when building analytics views (e.g. usage by feature or customer over time). Supports filtering, grouping, and time-series breakdown.
Use when batching usage events (e.g. backfill or high-volume ingestion). More efficient than single event calls; returns 202 when accepted.
Use when fetching Hugging Face inference usage or billing data (e.g. for HF-specific reporting or reconciliation).
Use when debugging ingestion or exporting raw event data (e.g. support or audit). Returns a paginated list; supports time range and sorting.
Use when building usage reports or dashboards across events. Supports filters and grouping; defaults to last 7 days if no range provided.
Use when showing usage for a specific meter (e.g. dashboard or overage check). Supports time range, filters, and grouping by customer or subscription.

get.description: Use when debugging a specific event (e.g. why it failed or how it was aggregated). Includes processing status and debug info.

post.description: Use when creating a manual or one-off invoice (e.g. custom charge or non-recurring billing). Invoice is created in draft; finalize when ready.
Use when showing a customer what they will be charged (e.g. preview before checkout or plan change). No invoice is created.
Use when listing or searching invoices (e.g. admin view or customer history). Returns a paginated list; supports filtering by customer, status, date range.

get.description: Use when loading an invoice for display or editing (e.g. portal or reconciliation). Supports group_by for usage breakdown and force_runtime_recalculation.

parameters.name: expand_by_source
parameters.description: Include source-level price breakdown for usage line items (legacy)

parameters.name: group_by
parameters.description: Group usage breakdown by specified fields (e.g., source, feature_id, properties.org_id)

put.description: Use when updating invoice metadata or due date (e.g. PDF URL, net terms). For paid invoices only safe fields can be updated.

post.description: Use when sending an invoice to the customer (e.g. trigger email or Slack). Payload includes full invoice details for your integration.
Use when locking an invoice for payment (e.g. after review). Once finalized, line items are locked; invoice can be paid or voided.

put.description: Use when reconciling payment status from an external gateway or manual entry (e.g. mark paid after bank confirmation).

post.description: Use when paying an invoice with the customer's wallet balance (e.g. prepaid credits or balance applied at checkout).

get.description: Use when delivering an invoice PDF to the customer (e.g. email attachment or download). Use url=true for a presigned URL instead of binary. Use force_generate=true to regenerate and re-upload the PDF even if one already exists in S3.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Return presigned URL from s3 instead of PDF

parameters.name: force_generate
parameters.description: Force regeneration of the PDF even if one already exists in S3 (default: false). Note: force_generate has no effect if invoice_pdf_url is already set on the invoice.

post.description: Starts an async workflow that creates a fresh replacement invoice for a voided SUBSCRIPTION invoice (same billing period). Returns workflow_id and run_id; poll workflow status or GET the new invoice via recalculated_invoice_id after completion.

400.description: Invalid request or invoice already recalculated

post.description: Recalculates a draft SUBSCRIPTION invoice in-place (replaces line items, reapplies credits/coupons/taxes). Use when subscription or usage data changed before finalizing.

parameters.name: finalize
parameters.description: Whether to finalize the invoice after recalculation (default: true)

post.description: Use when cancelling an invoice (e.g. order cancelled or duplicate). Only unpaid invoices can be voided.
Use when defining a new pricing plan (e.g. Free, Pro, Enterprise). Attach prices and entitlements; customers subscribe to plans.
Use when listing or searching plans (e.g. plan picker or admin catalog). Returns a paginated list; supports filtering and sorting.

get.description: Use when you need to load a single plan (e.g. for display or to create a subscription).

put.description: Use when changing plan details (e.g. name, interval, or metadata). Partial update supported.

delete.description: Use when retiring a plan (e.g. end-of-life). Existing subscriptions may be affected. Returns 200 with success message.

post.description: Clone an existing plan, copying its active prices, published entitlements, and published credit grants
Use when you have changed plan prices and need to push them to all active subscriptions (e.g. global price update). Returns workflow ID.
Use when adding a new price to a plan or catalog (e.g. per-seat, flat, or metered). Ideal for both simple and usage-based pricing.
Use when creating many prices at once (e.g. importing a catalog or setting up a plan with multiple tiers).

get.description: Use when resolving a price by external id (e.g. from your catalog or CMS). Ideal for integrations.

post.description: Use when listing or searching prices (e.g. plan builder or catalog). Returns a paginated list; supports filtering and sorting.

get.description: Use when you need to load a single price (e.g. for display or editing). Response includes expanded meter and price unit when applicable.

put.description: Use when changing price configuration (e.g. amount, billing scheme, or metadata).

delete.description: Use when retiring a price (e.g. end-of-life or replacement). Optional effective date or cascade for subscriptions.

post.description: Use when onboarding a customer to a plan or starting a new subscription. Ideal for draft subscriptions (activate later) or active from start.
Use when adding an optional product or add-on to an existing subscription (e.g. extra storage or support tier).

delete.description: Use when removing an add-on from a subscription (e.g. downgrade or opt-out).

put.description: Use when changing a subscription line item (e.g. quantity or price). Implemented by ending the current line and creating a new one for clean billing.

delete.description: Use when removing a charge or seat from a subscription (e.g. downgrade). Line item ends; retained for history but no longer billed.

post.description: Use when listing or searching subscriptions (e.g. admin view or customer subscription list). Returns a paginated list; supports filtering by customer, plan, status.
Use when showing usage for a subscription (e.g. in a portal or for overage checks). Supports time range and filters.

get.description: Use when you need to load a single subscription (e.g. for a billing portal or to check status).

put.description: Use when changing subscription details (e.g. quantity, billing anchor, or parent). Supports partial update; send "" to clear parent_subscription_id.

post.description: Use when turning a draft subscription live (e.g. after collecting payment or completing setup). Once activated, billing and entitlements apply.

get.description: Use when listing which add-ons are currently attached to a subscription (e.g. for display or editing).

post.description: Use when a customer churns or downgrades. Supports immediate or end-of-period cancellation and proration. Ideal for self-serve or support-driven cancellations.
Use when applying a plan change (e.g. upgrade or downgrade). Executes proration and generates invoice or credit as needed.
Use when showing a customer the cost of a plan change before they confirm (e.g. upgrade/downgrade preview with proration).

get.description: Use when checking what features or limits a subscription has (e.g. entitlement checks or feature gating). Optional feature_ids to filter.
Use when showing upcoming or pending credits for a subscription (e.g. in a portal or for forecasting).

post.description: Use when adding a new charge or seat to a subscription (e.g. extra seat or one-time add). Supports price_id or inline price.
Attach additional child customers (by external ID) to an active standalone or parent subscription; creates inherited skeleton subscriptions for each. The subscription must be active.
Use when temporarily stopping a subscription (e.g. customer hold or seasonal pause). Billing and access pause; resume when ready.

get.summary: List all pauses for a subscription
get.description: Use when showing pause history for a subscription (e.g. support or audit). Returns all past and future pauses.

post.description: Use when reactivating a paused subscription (e.g. end of hold). Billing and access resume from the resume date.

get.description: Use when you need a subscription with related data (line items, prices, plan). Supports expand for detailed payloads without extra round-trips.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of fields to expand (e.g., 'subscription_line_items,prices,plan')

get.description: Use when listing or searching scheduled changes across subscriptions (e.g. admin view). Returns schedules with optional filtering.
Use when you need to load a single scheduled change (e.g. to show when a plan change or renewal takes effect).

post.description: Use when cancelling a scheduled change (e.g. customer changed mind). Identify by schedule ID in path or by subscription ID + schedule type in body.

parameters.name: schedule_id
parameters.description: Schedule ID (optional if using request body)

requestBody.description: Cancel request (optional if using path parameter)

get.description: Use when listing scheduled changes for a subscription (e.g. upcoming plan change or renewal). Returns all schedules for that subscription.

ListCustomersResponse.description: Response object for listing customers with pagination

costsheetIDs.description: CostsheetIDs allows filtering by specific costsheet IDs

environmentID.description: EnvironmentID filters by specific environment ID

tenantID.description: TenantID filters by specific tenant ID

start_date.description: start_date is the new start date for the subscription when activating

line_item_commitments.description: LineItemCommitments allows setting commitment configuration per addon line item (keyed by price_id)
LineItemCommitments allows setting commitment configuration per addon line item (keyed by price_id)

period_end.description: period_end is the end of the new billing period

period_start.description: period_start is the start of the new billing period

schedule_id.description: schedule_id is the ID of the schedule to cancel (optional if subscription_id and schedule_type are provided)

subscription_id.description: subscription_id is the ID of the subscription (required if schedule_id is not provided)

reason.description: Reason for cancellation (for audit and business intelligence)

description.description: Description overrides the source plan's description when provided

display_order.description: DisplayOrder overrides the source plan's display order when provided

lookup_key.description: LookupKey is required and must be unique across published plans

name.description: Name is required and must be different from the source plan's name

conversion_rate.description:
amount in the currency =  number of credits * conversion_rate
ex if conversion_rate is 1, then 1 USD = 1 credit
ex if conversion_rate is 2, then 1 USD = 0.5 credits
ex if conversion_rate is 0.5, then 1 USD = 2 credits

topup_conversion_rate.description:
topup_conversion_rate is the conversion rate for the topup to the currency
ex if topup_conversion_rate is 1, then 1 USD = 1 credit
ex if topup_conversion_rate is 2, then 1 USD = 0.5 credits
ex if topup_conversion_rate is 0.5, then 1 USD = 2 credits

idempotency_key.description: idempotency_key is an optional key used to prevent duplicate credit note creation

process_credit_note.description: process_credit_note is a flag to process the credit note after creation

address_country.description: address_country is the two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code

name.description: name is the full name or company name of the customer

skip_onboarding_workflow.description:
skip_onboarding_workflow when true, prevents the customer onboarding workflow from being triggered
This is used internally when a customer is created via a workflow to prevent infinite loops
Default: false

tax_rate_overrides.description: tax_rate_overrides contains tax rate configurations to be linked to this customer

group_id.description: GroupID is the id of the group to add the feature to

idempotency_key.description: idempotency_key is an optional key used to prevent duplicate invoice creation

invoice_number.description: invoice_number is an optional human-readable identifier for the invoice

flat_amount.description:
flat_amount is the flat amount for the given tier (optional)
Applied on top of unit_amount*quantity. Useful for cases like "2.7$ + 5c"

unit_amount.description: unit_amount is the amount per unit for the given tier

up_to.description:
up_to is the quantity up to which this tier applies. It is null for the last tier.
IMPORTANT: Tier boundaries are INCLUSIVE.
- If up_to is 1000, then quantity less than or equal to 1000 belongs to this tier
- This behavior is consistent across both VOLUME and SLAB tier modes

base_currency.description: base_currency  is the currency that the price unit is based on

conversion_rate.description:
ConversionRate defines the exchange rate from this price unit to the base currency.
This rate is used to convert amounts in the custom price unit to the base currency for storage and billing.

Conversion formula:
  price_unit_amount * conversion_rate = base_currency_amount

Example:
  If conversion_rate = "0.01" and base_currency = "usd":
  100 price_unit tokens * 0.01 = 1.00 USD

Note: Rounding precision is determined by the base currency (e.g., USD uses 2 decimal places, JPY uses 0).

price_id.description: PriceID references an existing price (plan, addon, or subscription-scoped). Exactly one of price_id or price must be set.

addons.description: Addons represents addons to be added to the subscription during creation

commitment_amount.description: CommitmentAmount is the minimum amount a customer commits to paying for a billing period

credit_grants.description: Credit grants to be applied when subscription is created

customer_id is the flexprice customer id

external_customer_id is the customer id in your DB

amount in the currency =  number of credits * conversion_rate

initial_credits_expiry_date_utc is the expiry date in UTC timezone (optional to set nil means no expiry)
ex 2025-01-01 00:00:00 UTC

initial_credits_to_load.description:
initial_credits_to_load is the number of credits to load to the wallet
if not provided, the wallet will be created with 0 balance
NOTE: this is not the amount in the currency, but the number of credits

initial_credits_to_load_expiry_date.description:
initial_credits_to_load_expiry_date YYYYMMDD format in UTC timezone (optional to set nil means no expiry)
for ex 20250101 means the credits will expire on 2025-01-01 00:00:00 UTC
hence they will be available for use until 2024-12-31 23:59:59 UTC

price_unit is the code of the price unit to use for wallet creation
If provided, the price unit will be used to set the currency and conversion rate of the wallet:
- currency: set to price unit's base_currency
- conversion_rate: set to price unit's conversion_rate

ex if topup_conversion_rate is 0.5, then 1 USD = 2 credits

amount in the currency =  number of credits * conversion_rate

property_filters.description: Property filters to filter the events by the keys in `properties` field of the event

billing_anchor.description:
BillingAnchor enables custom monthly billing periods for meter usage aggregation.

Usage guidelines:
- Only effective when WindowSize = "MONTH"
- For other window sizes (DAY, HOUR, WEEK), this field is ignored
- When nil, uses standard calendar months (1st to 1st)
- When provided, creates custom monthly periods (e.g., 5th to 5th)

Common use cases:
- Subscription billing periods that don't align with calendar months
- Customer-specific billing cycles (e.g., signed up on 15th)
- Multi-tenant systems with different billing anchor dates

Example: If BillingAnchor = "2024-03-05T14:30:45Z" and WindowSize = "MONTH":
  - March period: 2024-03-05 14:30:45 to 2024-04-05 14:30:45
  - April period: 2024-04-05 14:30:45 to 2024-05-05 14:30:45

BillingAnchor enables custom monthly billing periods for usage aggregation.

When to use:
- WindowSize = "MONTH" AND you need custom monthly periods (not calendar months)
- Subscription billing that doesn't align with calendar months
- Example: Customer signed up on 15th, so billing periods are 15th to 15th

When NOT to use:
- WindowSize != "MONTH" (ignored for DAY, HOUR, WEEK, etc.)
- Standard calendar-based billing (1st to 1st of each month)

Example values:
- "2024-03-05T14:30:45.123456789Z" (5th of each month at 2:30:45 PM)
- "2024-01-15T00:00:00Z" (15th of each month at midnight)
- "2024-02-29T12:00:00Z" (29th of each month at noon - handles leap years)

group_by_property.description:
GroupByProperty is the property name in event.properties to group by before aggregating.
When set, aggregation is applied per unique value of this property within each bucket,
then the per-group results are summed to produce the bucket total.

property_name.description: will be empty/ignored in case of COUNT

line_item_id.description: price_id used to match the line item

is_proration.description: is_proration indicates if this line item is a proration

period_end.description: period_end for this line item (if applicable)

period_start.description: period_start for this line item (if applicable)

invoice_level_discount.description: invoice_level_discount is the discount amount in invoice currency applied to all line items on the invoice.

line_item_discount.description: line_item_discount is the discount amount in invoice currency applied directly to this line item.

prepaid_credits_applied.description: prepaid_credits_applied is the amount in invoice currency reduced from this line item due to prepaid credits application.

usage_analytics.description: usage_analytics contains usage analytics for this line item (legacy - grouped by source)

usage_breakdown.description: usage_breakdown contains flexible usage breakdown for this line item (supports any grouping)

currency.description: currency is the currency for all amounts

due_date.description: due_date is when the invoice would be due

line_items.description: line_items contains preview of line items

subtotal.description: subtotal is the subtotal amount before taxes

tax_amount.description: tax_amount is the total tax amount

total.description: total is the total amount including taxes

adjustment_amount.description:
adjustment_amount is the total sum of credit notes of type "adjustment".
These are non-cash reductions applied to the invoice (e.g. goodwill credit, billing correction).

amount_due.description: amount_due is the total amount that needs to be paid for this invoice

amount_paid.description: amount_paid is the amount that has already been paid towards this invoice

amount_remaining.description: amount_remaining is the outstanding amount still owed on this invoice (calculated as amount_due minus amount_paid)

billing_period.description: billing_period describes the billing period this invoice covers (e.g., "January 2024", "Q1 2024")

billing_reason.description: billing_reason indicates why this invoice was generated (e.g., "subscription_billing", "manual_charge")

billing_sequence.description: billing_sequence is the sequential number indicating the billing cycle for subscription invoices

coupon_applications.description: coupon_applications contains the coupon applications associated with this invoice (overrides embedded field)

currency.description: currency is the three-letter ISO currency code (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP) that applies to all monetary amounts on this invoice

customer_id.description: customer_id is the ID of the customer who will receive this invoice

description.description: description is an optional description or notes about this invoice

due_date.description: due_date is the date when payment for this invoice is due

environment_id.description: environment_id is the ID of the environment this invoice belongs to (for multi-environment setups)

finalized_at.description: finalized_at is the timestamp when this invoice was finalized and made ready for payment

id.description: id is the unique identifier for this invoice

idempotency_key.description: idempotency_key is a unique key used to prevent duplicate invoice creation when retrying API calls

invoice_number.description: invoice_number is the human-readable invoice number displayed to customers (e.g., INV-2024-001)

invoice_pdf_url.description: invoice_pdf_url is the URL where customers can download the PDF version of this invoice

last_computed_at.description: last_computed_at is the timestamp when this invoice was last computed by ComputeInvoice

line_items.description: line_items contains the individual items that make up this invoice (overrides embedded field)

overpaid_amount.description: overpaid_amount is the amount overpaid if payment_status is OVERPAID (amount_paid - total)

paid_at.description: paid_at is the timestamp when this invoice was fully paid

period_end.description: period_end is the end date of the billing period covered by this invoice

period_start.description: period_start is the start date of the billing period covered by this invoice

recalculated_invoice_id.description:
recalculated_invoice_id is the ID of the replacement invoice created when this invoice was voided and recalculated.
When set, it forms a parent→child link from this (voided) invoice to the new replacement invoice.

refunded_amount.description:
refunded_amount is the total sum of credit notes of type "refund".
These are actual refunds issued to the customer.

subscription_id.description: subscription_id is the ID of the subscription this invoice is associated with (only present for subscription-based invoices)

subtotal.description: subtotal is the sum of all line items before any taxes, discounts, or additional fees

taxes.description: tax_applied_records contains the tax applied records associated with this invoice

total.description: total is the final amount including taxes, fees, and discounts

total_discount.description: total_discount is the sum of all coupon discounts applied to the invoice

total_prepaid_credits_applied.description: total_prepaid_credits_applied is the total amount of prepaid credits applied to this invoice.

total_tax.description: total_tax is the sum of all taxes combined at the invoice level.

version.description: version is the version number for tracking changes to this invoice

voided_at.description: voided_at is the timestamp when this invoice was voided or cancelled

commitment_amount.description: CommitmentAmount is the minimum amount committed for this line item

commitment_quantity.description: CommitmentQuantity is the minimum quantity committed for this line item

enable_true_up.description: EnableTrueUp determines if true-up fee should be applied when usage is below commitment

is_window_commitment.description: IsWindowCommitment determines if commitment is applied per window (e.g., per day) rather than per billing period

overage_factor.description: OverageFactor is a multiplier applied to usage beyond the commitment

items.description:
List of subscription pause objects
@Description Array of subscription pauses

total.description:
Total number of pauses
@Description Total count of subscription pauses in the response

ListSubscriptionPausesResponse.description: Response object for listing subscription pauses with total count

entitlement_id.description: EntitlementID references the plan/addon entitlement to override

is_enabled.description: IsEnabled determines if the entitlement is enabled or disabled

static_value.description: StaticValue is the static value for static features

usage_limit.description:
UsageLimit is the new usage limit (only these 3 fields can be overridden)
For metered features, nil means unlimited usage

amount.description: Amount is the new price amount that overrides the original price (optional)

price_id.description: PriceID references the plan price to override

price_unit_amount.description: PriceUnitAmount is the amount of the price unit (for CUSTOM type, FLAT_FEE/PACKAGE billing models)

price_unit_tiers.description: PriceUnitTiers are the tiers for the price unit (for CUSTOM type, TIERED billing model)

tiers.description: Tiers determines the pricing tiers for this line item

PauseSubscriptionRequest.description: Request object for pausing an active subscription with various pause modes and options

amount.description:
Amount stored in main currency units (e.g., dollars, not cents)
For USD: 12.50 means $12.50

billing_period_count.description: BillingPeriodCount is the count of the billing period ex 1, 3, 6, 12

conversion_rate.description: ConversionRate is the conversion rate of the price unit to the fiat currency

currency.description: Currency 3 digit ISO currency code in lowercase ex usd, eur, gbp

display_amount.description:
DisplayAmount is the formatted amount with currency symbol
For USD: $12.50

display_name.description: DisplayName is the name of the price

display_price_unit_amount.description: DisplayPriceUnitAmount is the formatted amount of the price unit

end_date.description: EndDate is the end date of the price

entity_id.description: EntityID holds the value of the "entity_id" field.

environment_id.description: EnvironmentID is the environment identifier for the price

group_id.description: GroupID references the group this price belongs to

id.description: ID uuid identifier for the price

lookup_key.description: LookupKey used for looking up the price in the database

meter_id.description: MeterID is the id of the meter for usage based pricing

min_quantity.description: MinQuantity is the minimum quantity of the price

parent_price_id.description: ParentPriceID references the root price (always set for price lineage tracking)

price_unit.description: PriceUnit is the code of the price unit (e.g., 'btc', 'eth')

price_unit_amount.description: PriceUnitAmount is the amount of the price unit

price_unit_id.description: PriceUnitID is the id of the price unit (for CUSTOM type)

price_unit_tiers.description: PriceUnitTiers are the tiers for the price unit when BillingModel is TIERED

start_date.description: StartDate is the start date of the price

trial_period_days.description:
TrialPeriod is the number of days for the trial period
Note: This is only applicable for recurring prices (BILLING_CADENCE_RECURRING)

charge_amount.description: charge_amount is the charge amount for the new subscription

charge_description.description: charge_description describes what the charge is for

credit_amount.description: credit_amount is the credit amount from the old subscription

credit_description.description: credit_description describes what the credit is for

currency.description: currency is the currency for all amounts

current_period_end.description: current_period_end is the end of the current billing period

current_period_start.description: current_period_start is the start of the current billing period

days_remaining.description: days_remaining is the number of days remaining in the current period

days_used.description: days_used is the number of days used in the current period

net_amount.description: net_amount is the net amount (charge - credit)

proration_date.description: proration_date is the date used for proration calculations

percentage.description: percentage is the percentage of total line item cost from this source (optional)

credit_grants.description: credit_grants contains any credit grants created for proration credits

is_scheduled.description: is_scheduled indicates if the change was scheduled or executed immediately

warnings.description: warnings contains any warnings about the change

auto_apply.description: Whether this tax should be automatically applied

metadata.description: Metadata holds the value of the "metadata" field.

priority.description: Priority for tax resolution (lower number = higher priority)

amount.description:
amount is the amount in the currency of the wallet to be added
NOTE: this is not the number of credits to add, but the amount in the currency
amount = credits_to_add * conversion_rate
if both amount and credits_to_add are provided, amount will be ignored
ex if the wallet has a conversion_rate of 2 then adding an amount of
10 USD in the wallet wil add 5 credits in the wallet

credits_to_add.description: credits_to_add is the number of credits to add to the wallet

description.description: description to add any specific details about the transaction

expiry_date_utc.description:
expiry_date_utc is the expiry date in UTC timezone
ex 2025-01-01 00:00:00 UTC

idempotency_key.description: idempotency_key is a unique key for the transaction

priority.description:
priority is the priority of the transaction
lower number means higher priority
default is nil which means no priority at all

invoice_id.description: Invoice ID if an invoice was created (only for PURCHASED_CREDIT_INVOICED)

address_city.description: address_city is the updated city name with maximum 100 characters

address_country.description: address_country is the updated two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code

address_line2.description: address_line2 is the updated secondary address line with maximum 255 characters

address_postal_code.description: address_postal_code is the updated postal code with maximum 20 characters

address_state.description: address_state is the updated state, province, or region name with maximum 100 characters

integration_entity_mapping.description: integration_entity_mapping contains provider integration mappings for this customer

metadata.description: metadata contains updated key-value pairs that will replace existing metadata

name.description: name is the updated name or company name for the customer

GroupID is the id of the group to update the price in.
If not provided (nil), the group will not be changed

If provided as a group ID, the price will be assigned to that group (must exist and be published)

All price fields that can be updated

conversion_rate.description:
amount in the currency =  number of credits * conversion_rate
ex if conversion_rate is 1, then 1 USD = 1 credit
ex if conversion_rate is 2, then 1 USD = 0.5 credits
ex if conversion_rate is 0.5, then 1 USD = 2 credits

topup_conversion_rate.description:
topup_conversion_rate is the conversion rate for the topup to the currency
ex if topup_conversion_rate is 1, then 1 USD = 1 credit
ex if topup_conversion_rate is 2, then 1 USD = 0.5 credits
ex if topup_conversion_rate is 0.5, then 1 USD = 2 credits

conversion_rate.description:
amount in the currency =  number of credits * conversion_rate
ex if conversion_rate is 1, then 1 USD = 1 credit
ex if conversion_rate is 2, then 1 USD = 0.5 credits
ex if conversion_rate is 0.5, then 1 USD = 2 credits

topup_conversion_rate is the conversion rate for the topup to the currency
ex if topup_conversion_rate is 1, then 1 USD = 1 credit
ex if topup_conversion_rate is 2, then 1 USD = 0.5 credits
ex if topup_conversion_rate is 0.5, then 1 USD = 2 credits

topup_conversion_rate.description: topup_conversion_rate is the conversion rate for the topup to the currency

filters.description: filters allows complex filtering based on multiple fields
filters allows complex filtering based on multiple fields
filters allows complex filtering based on multiple fields
filters allows complex filtering based on multiple fields

amount_due_gt.description:
amount_due_gt filters invoices with a total amount due greater than the specified value
Useful for finding invoices above a certain threshold or identifying high-value invoices

amount_remaining_gt.description:
amount_remaining_gt filters invoices with an outstanding balance greater than the specified value
Useful for finding invoices that still have significant unpaid amounts

customer_id.description:
customer_id filters invoices for a specific customer using FlexPrice's internal customer ID
This is the ID returned by FlexPrice when creating or retrieving customers

external_customer_id.description:
external_customer_id filters invoices for a customer using your system's customer identifier
This is the ID you provided when creating the customer in FlexPrice

invoice_ids.description:
invoice_ids restricts results to invoices with the specified IDs
Use this to retrieve specific invoices when you know their exact identifiers

invoice_status.description:
invoice_status filters by the current state of invoices in their lifecycle
Multiple statuses can be specified to include invoices in any of the listed states

payment_status.description:
payment_status filters by the payment state of invoices
Multiple statuses can be specified to include invoices with any of the listed payment states

period_end_gte.description: period_end_gte filters invoices with period_end >= value

period_end_lte.description: period_end_lte filters invoices with period_end <= value

period_start_gte.description: period_start_gte filters invoices with period_start >= value

period_start_lte.description: period_start_lte filters invoices with period_start <= value

skip_line_items.description: SkipLineItems if true, will not include line items in the response

subscription_id.description:
subscription_id filters invoices generated for a specific subscription
Only returns invoices that were created as part of the specified subscription's billing

filters.description: filters allows complex filtering based on multiple fields
filters allows complex filtering based on multiple fields

x-enum-comments.ProrationBehaviorNone: Calculate but don't apply (e.g., for previews)

conversion_rate.description: Multiplier: reporting_unit_value = unit_value * conversion_rate; must be > 0

endpoint_url.description: Custom S3 endpoint URL (e.g., "http://minio:9000" for MinIO)

use_path_style.description: Use path-style addressing instead of virtual-hosted-style (required for MinIO)

active_at.description: ActiveAt filters subscriptions that are active at the given time

effective_date_for_update.description:
EffectiveDateForUpdate selects subscriptions that need a billing-period pass on or before this time:
current_period_end <= date OR (cancel_at IS NOT NULL AND cancel_at <= date).
When nil, period/cancel cutoff logic is not applied by this field (see TimeRangeFilter for legacy period-end filtering).

external_customer_id.description: ExternalCustomerID filters by external customer ID

invoicing_customer_ids.description: InvoicingCustomerIDs filters by invoicing customer ID

parent_subscription_ids.description: ParentSubscriptionIDs filters by parent subscription IDs

with_line_items.description: WithLineItems includes line items in the response

filters.description: filters allows complex filtering based on multiple fields

allowed_price_types.description:
AllowedPriceTypes is a list of price types that are allowed for the wallet
nil means all price types are allowed

filters.description: filters allows complex filtering based on multiple fields
filters allows complex filtering based on multiple fields (same as FeatureFilter)

invoice_level_discount.description: invoice_level_discount is the discount amount in invoice currency applied to all line items on the invoice.

line_item_discount.description: line_item_discount is the discount amount in invoice currency applied directly to this line item.

prepaid_credits_applied.description: prepaid_credits_applied is the amount in invoice currency reduced from this line item due to prepaid credits application.

expression.description:
Expression is an optional CEL expression to compute per-event quantity from event.properties.
When set, it replaces Field-based extraction. Property names are used directly (e.g., token * duration * pixel).

field.description:
Field is the key in $event.properties on which the aggregation is to be applied
For ex if the aggregation type is sum for API usage, the field could be "duration_ms"
Ignored when Expression is set.

group_by.description:
GroupBy is the property name in event.properties to group by before aggregating.
Currently only supported for MAX aggregation with bucket_size.
When set, aggregation is applied per unique value of this property within each bucket,
then the per-group results are summed to produce the bucket total.

multiplier.description:
Multiplier is the multiplier for the aggregation
For ex if the aggregation type is sum_with_multiplier for API usage, the multiplier could be 1000
to scale up by a factor of 1000. If not provided, it will be null.

key.description:
Key is the key for the filter from $event.properties
Currently we support only first level keys in the properties and not nested keys

values.description:
Values are the possible values for the filter to be considered for the meter
For ex "model_name" could have values "o1-mini", "gpt-4o" etc

environment_id.description: EnvironmentID is the environment identifier for the meter

event_name.description:
EventName is the unique identifier for the event that this meter is tracking
It is a mandatory field in the events table and hence being used as the primary matching field
We can have multiple meters tracking the same event but with different filters and aggregation

filters.description:
Filters define the criteria for the meter to be applied on the events before aggregation
It also defines the possible values on which later the charges will be applied

id.description: ID is the unique identifier for the meter

name.description: Name is the display name of the meter

amount.description:
Amount stored in main currency units (e.g., dollars, not cents)
For USD: 12.50 means $12.50

billing_period_count.description: BillingPeriodCount is the count of the billing period ex 1, 3, 6, 12

conversion_rate.description: ConversionRate is the conversion rate of the price unit to the fiat currency

currency.description: Currency 3 digit ISO currency code in lowercase ex usd, eur, gbp

display_amount.description:
DisplayAmount is the formatted amount with currency symbol
For USD: $12.50

display_name.description: DisplayName is the name of the price

display_price_unit_amount.description: DisplayPriceUnitAmount is the formatted amount of the price unit

end_date.description: EndDate is the end date of the price

entity_id.description: EntityID holds the value of the "entity_id" field.

environment_id.description: EnvironmentID is the environment identifier for the price

group_id.description: GroupID references the group this price belongs to

id.description: ID uuid identifier for the price

lookup_key.description: LookupKey used for looking up the price in the database

meter_id.description: MeterID is the id of the meter for usage based pricing

min_quantity.description: MinQuantity is the minimum quantity of the price

parent_price_id.description: ParentPriceID references the root price (always set for price lineage tracking)

price_unit.description: PriceUnit is the code of the price unit (e.g., 'btc', 'eth')

price_unit_amount.description: PriceUnitAmount is the amount of the price unit

price_unit_id.description: PriceUnitID is the id of the price unit (for CUSTOM type)

price_unit_tiers.description: PriceUnitTiers are the tiers for the price unit when BillingModel is TIERED

start_date.description: StartDate is the start date of the price

trial_period_days.description:
TrialPeriod is the number of days for the trial period
Note: This is only applicable for recurring prices (BILLING_CADENCE_RECURRING)

flat_amount.description:
flat_amount is the flat amount for the given tier (optional)
Applied on top of unit_amount*quantity. Useful for cases like "2.7$ + 5c"

unit_amount.description: unit_amount is the amount per unit for the given tier

up_to.description:
up_to is the quantity up to which this tier applies. It is null for the last tier.
IMPORTANT: Tier boundaries are INCLUSIVE.
- If up_to is 1000, then quantity less than or equal to 1000 belongs to this tier
- This behavior is consistent across both VOLUME and SLAB tier modes

environment_id.description: EnvironmentID is the environment identifier for the pause

id.description: ID is the unique identifier for the subscription pause

original_period_end.description: OriginalPeriodEnd is the end of the billing period when the pause was created

original_period_start.description: OriginalPeriodStart is the start of the billing period when the pause was created

pause_end.description: PauseEnd is when the pause will end (null for indefinite)

pause_start.description: PauseStart is when the pause actually started

reason.description: Reason is the reason for pausing

resumed_at.description: ResumedAt is when the pause was actually ended (if manually resumed)

subscription_id.description: SubscriptionID is the identifier for the subscription

end_date.description: EndDate is when the phase ends (nil if phase is still active or indefinite)

environment_id.description: EnvironmentID is the environment identifier for the phase

id.description: ID is the unique identifier for the subscription phase

start_date.description: StartDate is when the phase starts

subscription_id.description: SubscriptionID is the identifier for the subscription

ApiKeyAuth.description: Enter your API key in the format *x-api-key <api-key>**
ApiKeyAuth.name: x-api-key
